content:
Consider the following Python declarations of two lists.

$$ \begin{aligned} & A=[1,2,3] \\ & B=[4,5,6] \end{aligned} $$

Which one of the following statements results in $A=[1,2,3,4,5,6]$ ?
explanation:
Thus is a fact based question related to properties of Python.

So, A extend B is correct.

Hence, the correct option is (A).

content:
Consider the following Python code snippet.

$\mathrm{A}=\{$ "this","that" $\}$

$B=\{$ "that","other" $\}$

$\mathrm{C}=\{$ "other","this"}

while "other" in C :

if "this" in A :

$\mathrm{A}, \mathrm{B}, \mathrm{C}=\mathrm{A}-\mathrm{B}, \mathrm{B}-\mathrm{C}, \mathrm{C}-\mathrm{A}$

if "that" in B ;

$\mathrm{A}, \mathrm{B}, \mathrm{C}=\mathrm{C}|\mathrm{A}, \mathrm{A}| \mathrm{B}, \mathrm{B} \mid \mathrm{C}$

When the above program is executed, at the end, which of the following sets contains "this"?
explanation:
After first iteration of while loop

$$ \begin{aligned} & A=\{\text { this, other }\} \\ & B=\{\text { that }, \text { this }\} \\ & C=\{\text { other }, \text { that }\} \end{aligned} $$

After second iteration of while loop

$$ \begin{aligned} & A=\{\text { other }\} \\ & B=\{\text { this }\} \\ & C=\{\text { that }\} \end{aligned} $$

B set contain "this"

Hence, the correct option is (B).

content:
Consider the following Python code snippet.

$\operatorname{def} f(a, b)$ :

if ( $a==0$ ):

return b

$$ \begin{aligned} &\begin{aligned} & \text { if }(\mathrm{a} \% 2==1) \text { : } \\ & \text { return } 2 * f((a-1) / 2, b) \\ & \text { return } b+f(a-1, b) \\ & \operatorname{print}(f(15,10)) \end{aligned}\\ &\text { The value printed by the code snippet is__________ } \end{aligned} $$

Consider the following pseudocode.

Create empty stack S

set $x=0$, flag $=0$, sum $=0$

Push $x$ onto $S$

while ( $S$ is not empty) $\{$

if (flag equals 0){

Set $x=x+1$

Push $x$ onto S}

if ( $x$ equals 8 ):

Set flag $=1$

if (flag equals 1){

$x=\operatorname{Pop}(\mathrm{S})$

if ( $x$ is odd):

Pop (S)

Set sum $=\operatorname{sum}+\mathrm{x}\}$

}

Output sum

The value of sum output by a program executing the above pseudocode is________
explanation:
$$ \begin{array}{|c|} \hline 8 \\ \hline 7 \\ \hline 6 \\ \hline 5 \\ \hline 4 \\ \hline 3 \\ \hline 2 \\ \hline 1 \\ \hline 0 \\ \hline \end{array} $$

By running the while loop.

$$ x \rightarrow 0 \quad 1 \quad 2 \quad 39 $$

$$ \begin{aligned} \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,& 5 \\ & 6 \\ & 7 \\ & 8 \end{aligned} $$

$$ \begin{aligned} &\begin{aligned} & \text { Flag } \rightarrow 1 \\ & \text { As } x=8 \\ & \text { Sum }=8+7+5+3+1=24 \end{aligned}\\ &\text { Hence, the correct answer is } 24 \text {. } \end{aligned} $$
